summary: Excellent soundboard transfer of a 1973 show brimming with vocal harmony and a legendary Half‑Step > Playin' > El Paso > Wharf Rat sequence.
review: The Miller‑fixed SBD captures one of the strongest late‑’73 performances, highlighted by soaring vocals on Jack Straw, Here Comes Sunshine and an unforgettable Mississippi Half‑Step → Playin' → El Paso → Wharf Rat → Morning Dew suite.  The recording is crisp with expertly spliced audience patches, though track labeling is sparse; musically the band is in top form, even if the Me & My Uncle opener feels a little awkward.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 15 reviews are 4‑5 stars and repeatedly praise both the show’s musical peaks and the high‑quality soundboard transfer; only a few minor criticisms appear.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced SBD with careful audience patch splicing (Charlie Miller transfer). No audible dropouts, though track/label information is missing.

standout_songs: Mississippi Half Step > Playing in the Band > El Paso > Playing in the Band > Wharf Rat > Morning Dew
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds nicely with strong vocal harmonies and solid rockers; the second set erupts into a jazzy, space‑filled marathon that showcases the band’s improvisational peak of late ’73.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for soaring tone, superb solos (Big River, Jack Straw) and being "beardless"—a fan favorite moment.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for deep, weaving bass lines on Here Comes Sunshine and overall tightness; called “Keith’s at his best” with Phil complementing the jams.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is highlighted for rhythmic drive in Dire Wolf and the jazzy band jams; several reviewers call his playing a key part of the show’s sparkle.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann gets specific shout‑outs (focus on Jerry and Bill) and is praised for solid support throughout the long second‑set improvisations.
